title: Cognitive biases in clinical reasoning
key_recommendations: Anchoring bias: clinging to initial impression despite later contradictory evidence. Mitigation - explicitly list 3 alternative diagnoses before finalizing.
Premature closure: stopping the differential too early. Mitigation - 'what else could this be?' as a hard checkpoint.
Availability bias: recent or memorable cases inflate perceived probability. Mitigation - reason from base rates first.
Confirmation bias: weighting evidence that supports the working dx. Mitigation - actively search for refuting evidence.
Iatrogenesis blindness: failure to consider 'is this caused by something we did?' Mitigation - in any new symptom, review the med list within 30 days of onset.
